responsibilities: Developed robust, cloud-integrated microservices in Python, utilizing advanced orchestration to optimize execution speeds and support the seamless scaling of data-intensive operations.
Architected a scalable, stateful agentic system using LangGraph and Python, integrating multi-step RAG workflows with GPT-4 and Claude Haiku to process complex operational data with 99.9% uptime.
Implemented automated monitoring via Prometheus and Grafana to track real-time data and model drift, maintaining a variance of less than 2% across production environments.

Optimized high-scale search infrastructure by architecting a hybrid-cloud vector retrieval layer (Pinecone/FAISS), achieving a 40% reduction in operational spend and a 22% decrease in latency; implemented semantic caching to eliminate redundant LLM processing.

Increased modularity by 35% by decoupling a monolithic RAG pipeline using MCP, increasing system debugging efficiency by 20%, and unifying RAG access to predictive models (LSTMs, XGBoost) for analytical forecasting.

Orchestrated distributed data pipelines using Ray and Airflow to parallelize chunking and vectorization, reducing metadata processing latency by 50% and improving retrieval quality by 25% through optimized indexing.

Architected agentic frameworks on Amazon Bedrock and AWS EKS, identified operational bottlenecks in memory retrieval, and implemented a Terraform-led IaC strategy to automate infrastructure, reducing CPU utilization by 40% and reducing operational time using GitHub Actions by half.

Engineered a high-velocity WAMI detection pipeline by integrating ReYOLOv8, ConvLSTM, and VTEI using LoRA transfer learning; optimized spatiotemporal tracking for high-speed targets while maintaining minimal memory overhead.

Invented a high-fidelity simulation framework by integrating DroneWIS with Matrix City (CARLA/AirSim), eliminating 90% of physical testing overhead; leveraged C++/Python to model complex aerodynamic constraints for large-scale synthetic data generation.
Developed a wide-area mapping system utilizing SIFT, SURF, and LDH algorithms to enable seamless feature alignment and real-time panoramic reconstruction across distributed drone swarms

Optimized model performance by implementing GPU-based quantization, accelerating inference and training speeds by 1.5x while maintaining high accuracy for real-time spatiotemporal object tracking in simulated environments.

Engineered a high-performance recommendation engine using Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) and Factorization Machines, delivering personalized content to a million active users. Achieved a 20% increase in user engagement by optimizing real-time inference through a Java-based microservices layer.
Architected scalable Apache Beam ETL pipelines for Snowflake ingestion and optimized FastAPI, GraphQL, and SQLAlchemy query patterns, reducing model data retrieval latency by 25%.
Deployed Spring Boot microservices on GKE using pod locality tuning and private networking, cutting API latency by 30%; utilized Cloud Monitoring to resolve bottlenecks within Kafka consumer groups.
Enhanced system reliability by authoring technical design docs and runbooks, reducing MTTR by 25% through automated MLflow/Jenkins MLOps pipelines.

projects.name: Farmer Collaboration Communities (Agricom)
description: Architected a high-availability agri-tech platform using the MEAN stack, optimizing Node.js services and MongoDB query performance to reduce REST API latency by 30%; implemented TLS/SSL encryption and secure JWT-based authentication to ensure data integrity during real-time information exchange.

projects.name: Wide Area Motion Imagery captured using a multi-fleet UAV virtual camera. (Thesis Project)
description: Engineered a multi-drone imaging pipeline in AirSim utilizing visual servoing and custom C++/Python controllers for synchronized swarm flight; integrated LDH (Light-Field Dynamic Homography) and SIFT/SURF feature matching to achieve seamless wide-area stitching, reducing Mean Pixel Error (MPE) to 1/3 of baseline methods.

projects.name: Churn Prediction (Paper published on page no 37)
description: Developed a banking churn prediction framework by benchmarking Logistic Regression, KNN, and Naive Bayes against ensemble methods; benchmarked and deployed a Random Forest framework, achieving 80.35% accuracy to drive customer retention strategies; and engineered a feature selection pipeline from transaction metadata and EDA to mitigate overfitting and identify key behavioral triggers for customer retention.

education_list.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
